title: Hall of fame

infobox.value: is a list of individuals, achievements, or other entities, usually chosen by a group of electors, to mark their excellence, accomplishments and [fame](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/fame "wiktionary:fame") in their field. In some cases, these halls of fame consist of actual halls or museums that enshrine the honorees with sculptures, plaques, and displays of memorabilia and general information regarding the inducted recipients. Sometimes, the honorees' plaques may instead be posted on a wall (hence a "wall of fame") or inscribed on a sidewalk (as in a "walk of fame", "walk of stars", or "avenue of fame"). In other cases, the hall of fame is more figurative and consists of a list of names of noteworthy people and their achievements and contributions. The lists are maintained by an organization or community, and may be national, state, local, or private.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Hall of fame](/w/index.php?title=Hall_of_fame&redirect=no "Hall of fame"))

Several terms ending in "of fame" redirect here. For other uses, see [Hall of fame (disambiguation)](/wiki/Hall_of_fame_(disambiguation) "Hall of fame (disambiguation)"), [Walk of fame (disambiguation)](/wiki/Walk_of_fame_(disambiguation) "Walk of fame (disambiguation)"), and [Wall of fame (disambiguation)](/wiki/Wall_of_fame_(disambiguation) "Wall of fame (disambiguation)").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f5/Hollywood_Walk_of_Fame_-_Los_Angeles%2C_California%2C_USA_2022_24.jpg/250px-Hollywood_Walk_of_Fame_-_Los_Angeles%2C_California%2C_USA_2022_24.jpg)](/wiki/File:Hollywood_Walk_of_Fame_-_Los_Angeles,_California,_USA_2022_24.jpg)

[Hollywood Walk of Fame](/wiki/Hollywood_Walk_of_Fame "Hollywood Walk of Fame"), Los Angeles, California, U.S.

A **hall of fame**, **wall of fame**, or **walk of fame** is a list of individuals, achievements, or other entities, usually chosen by a group of electors, to mark their excellence, accomplishments and [fame](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/fame "wiktionary:fame") in their field. In some cases, these halls of fame consist of actual halls or museums that enshrine the honorees with sculptures, plaques, and displays of memorabilia and general information regarding the inducted recipients. Sometimes, the honorees' plaques may instead be posted on a wall (hence a "wall of fame") or inscribed on a sidewalk (as in a "walk of fame", "walk of stars", or "avenue of fame"). In other cases, the hall of fame is more figurative and consists of a list of names of noteworthy people and their achievements and contributions. The lists are maintained by an organization or community, and may be national, state, local, or private.

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=List_of_halls_and_walks_of_fame&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Etymology")]

The term "hall of fame" first appeared in German with the [Ruhmeshalle](/wiki/Ruhmeshalle_(Munich) "Ruhmeshalle (Munich)"), built in 1853 in [Munich](/wiki/Munich "Munich").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The [Walhalla memorial](/wiki/Walhalla_(memorial) "Walhalla (memorial)") in [Bavaria](/wiki/Bavaria "Bavaria") was conceived in 1807 and built between 1830 and 1842.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Inspired by the Ruhmeshalle, the English-language term was popularised in the United States by the [Hall of Fame for Great Americans](/wiki/Hall_of_Fame_for_Great_Americans "Hall of Fame for Great Americans"),[[2]](#cite_note-nyt-2007-10-12-2) a sculpture gallery dedicated in 1901.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Located in [the Bronx](/wiki/The_Bronx "The Bronx") in New York City, it is on the campus of [Bronx Community College](/wiki/Bronx_Community_College "Bronx Community College") (until 1973 the [University Heights](/wiki/University_Heights,_Bronx "University Heights, Bronx") campus of [New York University](/wiki/New_York_University "New York University")).[[2]](#cite_note-nyt-2007-10-12-2)

The meaning of *fame* has changed over the years, originally meaning 'renown' for achievement, as opposed to today's more common meaning of '[celebrity](/wiki/Celebrity "Celebrity")'.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
